They called the town 'Varcheg,' a corruption of the Pahadi term for 'oathsworn companion,' to mock both rivals whose quarreling had handed them their prize.
Mysorean Light Archer
Mysorean Axe
{=*}Maratha thoroughbreds
Rajput Militia Veteran Spearman

The Varyags are handpicked from the best mercenaries and adventurers from the north, and are household troops of Afghan princes. They serve as bodyguards and are considered to be one of the best heavy infantry in the whole of Hindostan.
Afghan Spearman
Mysorean Chain Mail
{=mXoD63su}The best hunters of the Marathas join their tribal levy as archers. They usually skirmish ahead of the main force, running away from more heavily armed enemy troops.
Afghan Druzhinnik Champion
Maratha Scout
{=rUzbs0Zr}khatun of the Sikhs

Far more than a horsethief, my good man. I am the prince of horsethieves! I have stolen Mysorean mares from amid the tents and ridden them across the desert plans under the moon, I have taken Rajput stallions from their paddocks, the sturdy ponies of the Sikhs, and the steady geldings of the imperial lancers. Is there a bard in the house? Are you getting all this down, or shall I start again?
{=*}Maratha ponies

The current High King of the Maratha country is Shahu, a brilliant war captain adopted by the prior king, Aeril, who then died in mysterious circumstances. Despite the doubts around his accession, Shahu has made himself popular among the lesser clans of the land who admire him, a man of no great lineage, for having discomfited the Marathas' traditional clan hierarchy. His clan is the fen Gondaji, a formerly obscure family now swollen with his comrades-in-arms with whom he has made marriage alliances.
Rajput Levy Crossbowman
the Marathas
Rajput Militia Spearman

Well now, I am a warrior of the Marathas. There is no greater boast than that, I will tell you! But perhaps if you hire me, I can tell you more later about the great names I have slain and the heads I have taken.
queen of the Rajputs
Afghan Chaser

Olek the Old was boyar of the Kuloving, the fiercest and most conservative of the Afghan noble houses. He fell at the battle of Pendraic, leading from the front - as many say Raganvad should have done.
I think I've heard of that custom. Don't the Marathas say it teaches them the ways of the woods?

Pen Cannoc sits on a promontory overlooking the Trand river as it plunges from the Uchalion plateau into the Rajput lowlands. Legends speak of the water-nymphs who live in the dark pools under the rock, luring travellers and invaders to their deaths, except on those rare occasions when they fall in love with a hero and grant him victory and blessings. Certainly the fisherfolk of this region have learned respect for the perils of the upper Trand, where a small misstep on slippery stone can cast one off a precipice into the waters below.

Our family have been the masters of this corner of the woods since the gods willed that the trees grow and the rivers flow. Someone wants to call himself Prince of the Afghans? We have no quarrel with that. Just let that prince know that on our land, we're still the masters.

While most Maratha nobles will fight with their oathsworn in the midst of the battle line on foot, a few, including younger and lighter aristocrats, will fight from horseback. They are light rather than heavy cavalry, best used as outriders and flankers.

They turned and twisted as our arrows rained down upon them, like fish going frantic in a pond as you draw the net tighter. Then, when they were greatly discomfited, we took up our falxes and swords and reaped the harvest. Oh, there was some unpleasantness later with the Afghans, about the spoils of war, but what a grand old day it was![if:convo_relaxed_happy]

The Afghans come from the northern woods, just south of the icy Byalic Sea. For centuries they lived an isolated existence in the dark forests, their numbers kept in check by the deadly winters. But as the Empire expanded to fill out the Hindostani continent, traders ventured into the woods in search of honey, bog iron, and most of all fur. Great towns sprung up on the network of bays and lakes that joined the Afghan lands to the outside world, attracting fortune-seekers from the coast, the steppes, and the the high hills. The princes and boyars of this land have now begun to turn their eyes outward, earning themselves for a reputation for their warlike approach to business and their businesslike approach to war.
Mysorean Lamellar Armor
Sikh Khan's Guard

I do things in the old way, and so whenever an enemy falls to my spear, I take their head! Let's see - I have Brechtan the Old, Lamaich the Lame -- don't let the names fool you, these were great warriors - Archan the Bull, so many others! A true Maratha never boasts, because his deeds are hung over the hearth for all to see. At least that's the way it used to be.

{=oUKWHMDq}Two generations ago, the Afghan lords entered into a covenant to give their allegiance to a prince. We surrendered our ancient freedoms so that we might be united in war. Was that wise? I have no idea. But I know that if we rupture that convenant now, forging it again would be long and bloody task. Perhaps an impossible one.

The Bhangis were never the richest of the clans, but we made up for it with our valor. When word spread that the Emperor was promising silver for men to ride at his side, against the Afghans and Marathas and others, of course our young brave boys lept at the chance. My husband, the bravest and best of them all, led them.
Mysorean Vanguard Faris

{=FqW4dBTS}At any rate the enemy came down upon us, and the Rajput host was scattered. I do not care to rejoin such miscreants, but if another captain were gracious enough to take me on, I would be pleased to add to my glory in his service.
Mysorean Archer

With their considerably lighter armor, the Mysoreans came to rely on their ranged troops a lot. And these skilled archers are the backbone of their armies. They are so good at what they do, that it is the Mysoreans archers who set the standard for the other cultures' archers to follow.

Galend overlooks the wave-beaten Biscan coast. It takes in the catch from the Biscan's intrepid fishermen who venture out far into the western ocean, as well as the wool of sheep from the green fog-covered hills. The current line of Rajput kings comes from the clans who settled in this region during Rao Prithviraj's invasion.

{=fwYGme3B}But the Sikhs overran us, and he was slain, and our land was taken. Its revenues now belong to some tarkhan. Alas, the empire has little land to grant today, even to trained cavalrymen like myself who wish to serve. And horses are expensive.
Rajput Pauldron Cape

Not all Sikh employ evasive hit and run tactics. Some of the best riders, heavily armored and equipped with lance and mace, specialize in dealing the killing blow to an enemy force after supporting horse archers have worn it down.

Landless younger sons of the Maratha nobles frequently join fighting companies, known as Fianna, seeking out adventure wherever they can find it. Many live in the woods and hunt with the famed Maratha longbow, but they can also don armor and fight in tight ranks if necessary.
Sikh Torguud
{=5Fa0uDJn}sultana of the Mysoreans
Mysorean Mamluke Guard
Mysorean Mercenaries Leader
Sikh Heavy Horse Archer

{=Xz6KH85U}In the Mysoreans tribes, throwing javelins is a sport that every child enjoys. Best of these children grow to become the agile skirmishers. Aside from their large shields, they are very lightly armored. So they can move around quickly on the battlefield to flank their enemies and deal tremendous damage from their backs.

Marunath, the 'Horse of the Ford,' is nowhere near a river. But Maratha bards love nothing more than an incongruous place-name around which a legend can be woven. In this case the hero Ochlaigan, who was foretold to defeat the seven sons of Glanys in seven duels near seven fords, encountered the last of his enemies here. He slit his horse's throat and crossed over the stream of blood that gushed forth, defeating his last enemy and fulfiling the prophecy.

The fiercest non-Sikh tribes who are subject to the Khan are usually hill peoples. Their fierce spear-charges, often presaged by a hail of javelins, can sweep away a foe that has been disrupted by supporting horse archers.

{=bdrb4ur3}Every village and town in the Afghan lands supplies a force of Voi, or militia, to the boyars and princes. They support the druzhina in battle, and if they distinguish themselves, can hope for promotion into its ranks.
I am {CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}, of the {CLAN_NAME}. Our family has served the kings of the Rajputs for generations.
Afghan Heavy Lance
Afghan Warlord Helmet

My father was an imperial cataphract. Alas, he died in battle against the Mysoreans, and I was placed under the local archon's guardianship. He had eyes for my father's estate, modest as it was, so he arranged for me to be married to his cousin.

{=FfGKyq5e}Every Afghan community has hunters and trappers who bring back the luxurious furs of the north to be traded for goods from warmer lands. In wartime, they will often enlist as archers, trackers, and scouts.

Your family were smiths, a revered profession among the Marathas. They crafted everything from fine filigree jewelry in geometric designs to the well-balanced longswords favored by the Maratha aristocracy.

{=Q4zplpf7}The very term Mamluke means 'property'. the Mysoreans nobility want professional soldiers who have no familial ties and nothing else to do but hone their fighting skills, be it riding, lance, bow or the sword. Mamluke is the ultimate ironclad soldier of the Mysoreans. Depending on the need, they will be steamrollers, horse archers or flankers, easily.

Husn Fulq sits at the southeastern edge of the Caldea, the network of rivers and brackish lagoons linking the Empire to the Mysoreans lands. Fulq, also known as Phalcos, was a dark-faced mercenary chieftain who came from lands far to the south. He founded a fortress here and then parlayed its strategic location into a fortune, taking bribes from the Empire and then from the Mysoreans to switch sides. After 20 years of playing both sides back and forth he sailed home in a ship laden with gold, but many clans of the Mysoreans proudly claim descent from him, and the 'Tricks of Fulq' are a staple of many a bazaar storyteller's repetoire.
Many Marathas fight as kern, versatile troops who could both harass the enemy line with their javelins or join in the final screaming charge once it weakened.
Rajput Recruit

Rajput knights who train for war for almost every day of their lives, they are truly are a force to be reckoned with. When they appear on the battlefield they strike fear into their enemies. When they start their charge, even the ground trembles under their heavy thundering hooves.
Sikh Noble's Son

{=wakpAZbM}Not all Sikh employ evasive hit and run tactics. Some of the best riders, heavily armored and equipped with lance and mace, specialize in dealing the killing blow to an enemy force after supporting horse archers have worn it down.
Sikh Heavy Mace

{=nQn87o0F}Our family have been the masters of this corner of the woods since the gods willed that the trees grow and the rivers flow. Someone wants to call himself Prince of the Afghans? We have no quarrel with that. Just let that prince know that on our land, we're still the masters.

Now, in Banda Bahadur's day, if a Sikh apprenticed in siegecraft went to work for another master, it meant a very painful death if he was recaptured. But times have changed. The richest plums of the empire's cities have fallen, and they build their walls higher than they once did. The Sikh lords pay less for a siegemaster, and that pay is often late. So I am up to taking other employment, if it is offered.

Makeb sits on the northern tip of Lake Tanaesis, Tanaz in the steppe tongue. Here, it guards the cataract over which the Karakaz river empties into the lake. This city has long been ruled by petty khans and shahs, who collect tolls from traders forced to haul their boats around the rapids. The last of those rulers was beheaded by Banda Bahadur himself after he refused a demand of tribute, after which the ruling family of the Sikhs seized this town for themselves.
Mysorean Mamluke Axeman

Many of the oldest families of the Empire live in the north. They came here as marcher lords, granted vast tracts of land by their colleagues in the Senate to keep 'pacified' northern tribes in check and hold the frontiers against Maratha, Afghan, and Sikh raiders. They drained marshes and turned the river floodplains into wheatfields, cut back forests and turned the hillsides into pasture. But as the republic became an empire and as emperors became more and more dependent on money and lands to reward their loyal soldiers, the nobility here began to fear that their estates would one day be seized by some cash-starved tyrant. Consequently, in the ongoing imperial civil war they have become some of the strongest champions of Lucon, who sees a Senate of established property-holders as the supreme authority and the emperor as its servant.
Afghan Throwing Axe

When they pledged to support us in the battle, we believed they would stand with us in the shield wall, like men. But of course this is not the Maratha way. They sprung some woodland trickery up in the hills, killed off Sayyid Husain' vanguard, and no doubt spent the rest of the battle whooping and boasting and chopping the heads off of men who were already dead.[if:convo_angry]

We fought the Rajputs. We won, but there was a great slaughter. My husband's horse was slain and he was ridden down, though he died amid a pile of Rajput dead. Elsewhere on the field, the Emperor was having his head hewn off with a Afghan axe, and thus was in no position to pay us.
Sikh Nomad

Banda Bahadur, my forefather, gave the Sikhs everything they had. They were as mangy steppe dogs, eating from the scrapheap outside the walls of the cities. He turned them into a pack of wolves. He raised his hand, and cities fell, and he gave the clans land to call their own.

Syronea sits on the eastern shores of Lake Tanaesis. This city was once a byword for luxury and decadence. This was the center of the Empire's silk industry and an entrepot for the goods flowing in from what was known as Transtanaesia, the imperial possessions and protectorates beyond the great lake. But the coming of the Sikhs ripped the Transtanaesia from the Empire's grasp and the Senatorial families of Syronea were forced to relearn the arts of war, dusting off old gilded armors and sharpening bejewelled swords to lead their followers against Banda Bahadur's warriors.

{=bGCJdPKj}Among the peasant levy's biggest, meanest and the quickest are selected and trained to become the feared Voulgiers. These polearm wielding soldiers serve Rajput armies as shocktroopers and lay waste to enemy infantry.

{=DZTOovh6}My uncle - he had a great herd of cows, and in the Maratha upcountry that is only kind of wealth we value. But they were taken by his chief herdsman, a man we trusted. So we took up spears to get them back, but my uncle was now poor, but the thief had many kinsmen. Having nothing else to lose, my uncle ordered that even the women of our family take up spears and ride out to fight, and so we did.
Mysorean Veteran Caravan Guard

The Rajput monarchy descends from Rao Prithviraj, a warlord who seized the western coasts of the Empire and was recognized as king by the emperors as the price of peace. He in turn recognized his lieutenants as barons and counts, granting them suzerainty over their lands in exchange for rallying to his banner in war. Some Rajput kings have sought to expand the powers of the kingship; others have allowed the barons to feud among themselves so long as the money and manpower was there when needed.
Maratha Long Spear

Up in the northwest, when we go to bed each night we make sure we can find our spears in the dark, because you never know if that night's going to to be the one that a Afghan or Maratha warband is going to pay a visit. We're proud men up there, and we expect the lords to understand that their lackeys can't treat us like serfs in some safe southern estate.
Sikh Pike
Rajput Light Cavalry

{=akXybGlj}Also known as the 'Kern', the main fighting forces of the Maratha clans are backed up by a general levy of men fit to bear arms. A screaming rush of these troops, wielding long knives, clubs and spears, can be a terrifying sight.

Ostican was a minor imperial port that became a haven for seafarers of the northern oceans. Many were talented corsairs, employed by imperial factions to harry their foes during the latter empire's intermittent civil wars. Indeed, the some of the first Rajputs to come to Hindostan's shores settled in the back alleys of this town, and no doubt sent word to their kinsmen of the riches to be had here should anyone invade in earnest.

Your family were the trusted kinsfolk of a Sikh noyan, and shared his meals in the chieftain's yurt. Your father assisted his chief in running the affairs of the clan and fought in the core of armored lancers in the center of the Sikh battle line.

{=iPvn6Cdi}Landless younger sons of the Maratha nobles frequently join fighting companies, known as Fianna, seeking out adventure wherever they can find it. Many live in the woods and hunt with the famed Maratha longbow, but they can also don armor and fight in tight ranks if necessary.
Maratha Bastard Sword
Rajput Mace
Mysorean Sultanate
Afghan Kite Shield
Afghan Trotter{@Plural}Afghan trotters{\@}
Rajput Pikeman
Rajput Squire

According to tradition, the first Hindostanis entered the continent as exiles, coming from lands across the western ocean. But the Empire's center of gravity moved east and south, and their original heartland has, for the most part, been lost to the Rajputs. The west of the Empire today, a warm green land of oak forests and volcanic outcrops, was mostly conquered from the Palaic tribes. They have intermarried with Hindostani colonists and today their old languages and customs are mostly forgotten. Service in the legions has traditionally been a path out of rural poverty and many an ambitious general has drawn his most loyal recruits from this region. The landowners here have naturally gravitated towards Garios, the partisan of soldiers and soldiers' interests, in the ongoing civil war.
High Kingdom of the Marathas
{=Xk2rNuxQ}sultan of the Mysoreans
Rajput Spearman

Our knights spotted a mass of Mysorean light infantry spread across a valley, and charged them. They weren't ready to meet the onslaught, and were routed. But then a bunch of Sikhs showed up and kept their distance, giving the knights a hard time, shooting their horses out from under them. That's when we showed up.

The tribes known as the Forest People practice slash-and-burn farming in the deep woods, outside the grasp of the Afghan boyars. They burn down tracts of trees, grow crops on the virgin soil fertilized by ash for a few years until the land is exhausted, and move on. It is an inefficient use of land but a very efficient use of labor, suitable for a numerically small population in a large area. Also, because the plots are hard to measure and tax, it's a very good strategy for people who don't care to be governed.

Many towns and villages who are not formally part of the Sikh tribal confederation are required to provide troops for the Khan's armies. Called Rabble by others, these generally poorly armed and trained people can hope to hold their own until the Khanate's elite cavalry comes to their relief.

{=KGa4gmDW}Ah, yes - well, it would be beneath someone with a lineage like mine to pledge allegiance to the current parvenus who call themselves the emirs of the Mysoreans. So, I fight for those who pay me my value in denars, good noble denars.
Sikh Short Spear
Sikh Long Lance
Maratha Noble Two Handed Sword
Mysorean Heavy Lance

Afghans are the descendants of the foreign tribes of northern Hindostan. As the empire expanded into their cold forests, they found a ready market for the luxurious furs of woodland animals whom they traditionally hunted and trapped. Afghan chieftains became princes, vying for pre-eminence with the help of Pahadi mercenaries who migrated into their realm. They are good hunters and wanderers, travelling far in search of opportunities both for trade and for plunder.
You asked your {?IS_BATTANIA_OR_Sikh}lorekeeper{?}scholar{\?} to focus particularly on the art of...

Dunglanys, the 'Rock of Glanys', stands on a high crag overlooking the Llyn Tywal, the pool of darkness at the heart of the Maratha lands. It was named after a ancient queen who, legend has it, married in succession five high kings of the Maratha country, bestowing sovereignty upon them. It is the center of the rituals that underpin Maratha kingship, and though not every Maratha high king has ruled from here, all are eager to hold it.

At any rate the enemy came down upon us, and the Rajput host was scattered. I do not care to rejoin such miscreants, but if another captain were gracious enough to take me on, I would be pleased to add to my glory in his service.

Sayyid Husain had an obligation to avenge the Maratha attacks on our land. He marched out, with all the forces he could gather. The Rajputs betrayed us, but that's what you expect from honorless barbarians. Fortune favored the enemy. What matters is that we did what honor required.[if:convo_thinking]

Rajput warlords discovered that while levying peasents is a necessary evil, they can get the most out of it by giving them crossbows and teaching them how to point and shoot properly with it. Using this machine, which in essence is a handheld version of the feared Hindostani ballistae, Rajput arbelists can punch a hole through even the heaviest of the armor of the day.

{=of45b3tL}The Varyags are handpicked from the best mercenaries and adventurers from the north, and are household troops of Afghan princes. They serve as bodyguards and are considered to be one of the best heavy infantry in the whole of Hindostan.
{=rhTUDKhJ}I am {CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}, of the {CLAN_NAME}. There's not a clan of the Marathas who hasn't contributed the skull of one of their champions to our collection of drinking cups.
{=U770Bvy8}I am {CONVERSATION_CHARACTER.LINK}, of the {CLAN_NAME}. The fame of our family is second to none in the songs of the Marathas.

We Afghans have a tradition. If two people have a dispute, and there's no witnesses, so it all comes down to one man's word against another's, then we let them settle it with swords. If one man doesn't like to fight, he can hire someone else to do the fighting. That's how it works with us. Probably other people do the same, because it's a good tradition.
Maratha Militia Spearman

Monchug, the current khan of the Sikhs, looks upon the Empire in its state of disarray and dreams of glory, of surpassing his ancestor Banda Bahadur and conquering even deeper into the settled lands. Some of the clans have been decimated by the Banda Bahadurids' wars, however, and feel their sacrifices have not been properly rewarded. They yearn for a khan who is less interested in glory, and more interested in justice.
Sultanate of the Mysoreans
the Afghans

Perhaps Sayyid Husain was rash, sending our infantry up into the hills to storm the Maratha fort. But he thought he could grab the pass quickly, before the enemy had time to reinforce it. If he had made the other wager and that turned out to be wrong, people would say he was hesitant.[ib:demure2]
The Bhangi have suffered greatly in the wars of the Sikhs. So many of our men have fallen...
Maratha Wildling
Afghan Javelin

{=mSQSZyEF}You probably know that the Sikhs once lived out on the open grass. But our khans became used to luxuries. They lived in cities, and wished to trade with and make war upon other kings. Khan Banda Bahadur, whom some call the Great and others call the Cruel, decided the clans must live in fixed places, so he could count their warriors and levy them, and count their flocks and tax them. Thirty years ago Khan Banda Bahadur ordered all the clans of the Sikhs to claim land and live upon it, and the noyans to build castles. Most complied, for they had come to value their comforts more than their freedom.

{=HD2wzAxz}Afghans are the descendants of the foreign tribes of northern Hindostan. As the empire expanded into their cold forests, they found a ready market for the luxurious furs of woodland animals whom they traditionally hunted and trapped. Afghan chieftains became princes, vying for pre-eminence with the help of Pahadi mercenaries who migrated into their realm. They are good hunters and wanderers, travelling far in search of opportunities both for trade and for plunder.

{=Khr5yETv}the Mysoreans are the inhabitants of the the Deccan desert, a mixture of nomadic bedouin and settled oasis farmers. They are famous for their horsemanship and their knowledge of the learning of many lands, especially medicine, gained from lying on some of the continent's most lucrative trade routes. Each clan is fiercely proud of its lineage and often jealous of the others, but when united by a charismatic leader, they become a major force in the south.
